results.title: Addressing Capacity Constraints in Soil Disposal and Reusing Excavated Soil Waste
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that excavated materials from construction are routed to staging grounds on the mainland for reuse as reclamation infill. To address growing disposal capacity needs, a new reclamation project at Northern Tuas Basin has recently commenced, increasing available capacity. The Government continues to monitor the situation and will work with the industry to assess if further measures are required for excavated material disposal.

results.title: Accreditation of Clinics for Mental Health Treatments under Chronic Diseases Management Programme
results.summary: To submit MediSave claims for mental health treatments under the Chronic Diseases Management Programme (CDMP), General Practitioners must be accredited via the Mental Health General Practitioner Partnership (MHGPP) programme, requiring adequate training and experience. All qualified GPs may apply, covering conditions like depression and schizophrenia. Treatments outside the CDMP are not MediSave-claimable, though patients receive up to 75% subsidies at polyclinics and specialist outpatient clinics. Additionally, patients aged 60 and above can use Flexi-MediSave for these non-CDMP treatments and medications, subject to prevailing withdrawal limits.

results.title: Assessment of Appeals by Seniors Excluded from Silver Support Scheme and Possible Review on Criteria
results.summary: The Silver Support Scheme targets seniors with limited means, using household monthly income per person as a key eligibility indicator to ensure resources reach those with less support. While residing with working children typically indicates sufficient household resources, appeals are reviewed holistically on a case-by-case basis to account for unique circumstances, such as estrangement preventing financial support from children. The Central Provident Fund Board handles these appeals. In 2024, the qualifying per capita household income threshold was raised from $1,800 to $2,300, allowing more seniors, including some living with their children, to qualify. The Ministry will continue periodic reviews to ensure the scheme targets those who need it most.

results.title: Average Processing Time for Bus Travellers at Land Checkpoints
results.summary: At land checkpoint bus halls, QR code clearance averages 10 seconds while passport clearance takes approximately 13 seconds. Currently, all Automated Border Clearance System lanes support both methods, with QR-only designation reserved for specific operational needs rather than peak hours or demographic factors. The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) does not adjust lane percentages based on traveller demographics or app download rates, as segregating lanes for non-QR users can prolong processing times. ICA continues to encourage MyICA Mobile app adoption to leverage the speed of QR clearance while maintaining smooth operations and border security.

results.title: BTO Projects with Waiting Times Exceeding Five Years
results.summary: Currently, 12 of 120 Build-To-Order projects have waiting times exceeding five years, with Ulu Pandan Banks and River Peaks I and II nearing six years. Extended timelines stem from stringent environmental monitoring and challenging ground conditions requiring extensive piling and underground works. Applicants are informed of specific waiting times at launch, which vary by project attributes. To mitigate delays, HDB is deploying technology and artificial intelligence through standardised modularisation, robotics like painting robots, and digital platforms to streamline workflows. These measures aim to reduce wait times, evidenced by nearly 70% of flats launched in the past three years having waits of four years or less.

results.title: Breakdown of Personal Disposable Income, Savings and Savings Rate by Citizenship Status and Entity Type over Last 10 Years
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated that disaggregated data on personal disposable income, personal savings, and savings rates by citizenship status or entity type are not available. The Department of Statistics compiles these metrics only at an aggregate level using administrative and survey sources. Data are published quarterly since 1980 and accessible via the Department of Statistics website. Consequently, the specific breakdown requested by the Member cannot be provided as the underlying data does not exist in the requested format.

results.title: Breakdown of Persons under 18 Employed to Support Household Income by Education Type
results.summary: Approximately 0.2% of the resident workforce comprises residents aged 15 to 17, with roughly three-quarters of this group working while schooling, including internships or vacation jobs, and the remaining quarter fully engaged in work. The Minister noted that reasons for employment vary, ranging from skill-building to supplementing family income. Schools collaborate with community partners to support students with irregular attendance, while the Government provides financial assistance through ComCare for lower-income families. Additionally, vulnerable families with children under 21 receive extra support via ComLink+, which offers customised wraparound services through dedicated family coaches.

results.title: Cessation of Integrated Childcare Programme for Children with Mild Special Needs
results.summary: The Integrated Childcare Programme (ICCP) for children with mild special needs was discontinued in 2024 following an evaluation that concluded its cessation was necessary. This decision was driven by enhanced accessibility to direct Early Intervention (EI) support within preschools since 2012, including the expansion of the Development Support and Learning Support (DS-LS) programme and teacher coaching. While the Inclusive Support Programme (InSP), which offers integrated EI services for children requiring medium support, will scale to more preschools starting in 2026, a service gap exists between 2024 and 2026. To bridge this interim period, the Early Childhood and Development Agency mandated that every preschool appoint an Inclusion Coordinator by 2023 to identify children needing EI and refer them to relevant resources.

results.title: Clarification by Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth corrected a previously stated date for the Notice of Intention (NOI). The official record confirms the NOI was issued on 3 November 2025, aligning with the Ministerial Statement. This clarification ensures accuracy regarding the timeline for the relevant initiative. No other policy changes or eligibility conditions were mentioned in this specific correction.

results.title: Clarification by Minister for National Development
results.summary: Minister Chee Hong Tat clarified that his previous remarks regarding GIC and Temasek investing in local equities were made during a closed-door DBS dialogue, not in Parliament. He confirmed that the Government does not support mandating these sovereign wealth funds to pump-priming the market, aligning with the Workers' Party's position against such directives. Consequently, Minister Chee formally withdrew the potentially misleading comments from the official record to ensure clarity. The parliamentary sitting was suspended at 3:43 pm and resumed at 4:05 pm following this exchange.

results.title: Clarification by Minister, Prime Minister's Office
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Singapore's ranking for total public expenditure on education as a percentage of GDP improved from 65th in 2024 to 63rd in 2025, correcting a previous statement. Regarding perception-based indicators, the ranking for primary and secondary education fell from second to third, while the university education ranking dropped from second to seventh between 2024 and 2025. Despite these slight declines, the Minister affirmed that all rankings remain high. The clarification addresses Ms Denise Phua's supplementary questions on the International Institute for Management Development's World Talent ranking.

results.title: Complaints Lodged by Third-party Lift Contractors on OEM Vendors' Non-adherence to Voluntary Commitments for Repairs and Warranty Periods
results.summary: The Building Construction Authority and Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) report no feedback regarding Original Equipment Manufacturer vendors' non-adherence to voluntary commitments since 2019. CCS will investigate and take necessary action if complaints are lodged by third-party lift contractors concerning these commitments. No specific improvements in pricing, delivery times, or warranty periods have been observed or stated. The Minister clarified that enforcement actions depend on the receipt of specific complaints rather than proactive monitoring of voluntary adherence.

results.title: Complaints against Foreign Confinement Nannies for Past Two Years and Mandatory Safety Protocols and Regulations for Work Permit Applications
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) reported receiving zero complaints regarding safety, fraud, or negligence involving foreign confinement nannies in 2023 and 2024. MOM regulates foreign employment through Work Passes, subjecting workers to labour laws and prevailing standards set by relevant authorities. The Minister did not confirm plans to introduce mandatory safety protocols for Work Permit applications, noting that existing regulations already govern the sector. Consequently, no new mandatory safety protocols or specific licensing requirements for confinement nannies were announced during this session.

results.title: Compliance Rates in Respective Progressive Wage Model Sectors
results.summary: Approximately 80% of eligible employees in Progressive Wage Model sectors received salaries meeting wage requirements. The Ministry of Manpower does not track compliance regarding training or working hours caps. To improve adherence, MOM will conduct targeted inspections in low-compliance sectors and collaborate with tripartite partners and sectoral leads to educate employers and assist them in meeting PWM obligations.

results.title: Congestion along Kovan-Serangoon Corridor Given New BTO Project
results.summary: Road improvement works are scheduled at the Tampines Road and Upper Serangoon Road junction to coincide with the Kovan Wellspring BTO development. The Kovan MRT station currently operates with 12 bus services. To mitigate peak period crowding, free morning off-peak rail rides on the North East Line will commence from end-December 2025. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these measures address congestion concerns arising from the new housing project but did not specify a separate timeline for road improvements beyond the concurrent development schedule.

results.title: Considerations for Land Area Proportion for Roads at Car-Lite Zoned Public Housing Estates
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that the Government's definition of car-lite zoned public housing estates does not establish a fixed land area proportion for roads. Instead, road area allocation varies across estates based on the specific type and scale of all land uses within each development. Consequently, these estates generally feature more bus lanes, dedicated cycling paths, and wider footpaths rather than a standardized road width requirement.

results.title: Current Regulations and Guidelines on Overtime Hours and Permissible Work Hours for Employees in Security Sectors
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower does not impose additional regulations on daily work hours or overtime compensation for security sector employees excluded from Part 4 of the Employment Act. These workers remain covered by other Employment Act parts regarding leave entitlements. Security agencies must strictly adhere to Singapore Police Force licensing conditions, limiting security officers to a maximum of 72 overtime hours per month. Agencies may also refer to Workplace Safety and Health Guidelines on Fatigue Management for recommended measures on rest breaks and shift duration limits.

results.title: Data on Processing Time for PDGE Applications and Withdrawal Rates Due to Prolonged Application Timelines in Last Three Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Education reported that shortlisted Postgraduate Diploma in Education (PGDE) applicants typically receive interview invitations within three to four weeks of the application window closing. Among early applicants selected after the interview, half received offers within two to five months, while approximately 7% of those who cleared the interview withdrew before receiving an offer. The Minister acknowledged that prolonged wait times may concern applicants and confirmed that the Ministry remains in contact with them throughout the process. MOE stated it is committed to maintaining a high-quality teaching workforce and will continue to streamline recruitment processes to improve efficiency and attract more candidates.

results.title: Data on Singaporeans in Critical Care Requiring Land-based Repatriation and Update on Foreign Private Ambulance Operators Accreditation Scheme
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not track the total number of land-based emergency ambulance repatriations of Singapore citizens from Malaysia or the average waiting times for these transfers. Regarding the Foreign Private Ambulance Operators Accreditation Scheme, MOH is currently assessing the feasibility of implementing such a scheme with relevant agencies. No specific data, timelines, or eligibility criteria for the accreditation scheme have been provided at this stage.

results.title: Decision-making Authority for Prosecution of Suspected Offences under Part 9 of Administration of Muslim Law Act
results.summary: The Attorney-General exercises independent prosecutorial discretion for suspected offences under Part 9 of the Administration of Muslim Law Act, with no requirement for express consent. The Minister clarified that internal review levels within Attorney-General's Chambers, such as Director or higher management, are operational matters determined independently by the Chambers based on case circumstances and public interest. Consequently, the specific authority level for decision-making and the classification of these offences as sensitive are not legislatively mandated but remain within the Attorney-General's Chambers' purview.

results.title: Demographic Statistics to Determine Intended Intake of New PRs and Citizens
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Permanent Resident and citizen intake considers multiple demographic indicators, including life expectancy and age profile, alongside total fertility rate and annual application volume. Starting with Population in Brief 2025, a technical reporting change excludes citizenships granted by descent from the total citizenship count to better reflect immigration inflows, as these are constitutional entitlements akin to birthright citizenship. Citizenships by descent will now be reported under the Marriage and Parenthood section and remain accessible via SingStat. The Minister confirmed this is a reporting adjustment only, with no change to the underlying intake determination methodology or expected impact on future intake levels.

results.title: Deployment of Agentic AI Systems in Civil Service and HR Policies to Support Officers Made Redundant
results.summary: The Government is adopting a careful approach to deploying agentic AI systems in the public service due to potential cascading risks. While these AI tools can automate actions like sending emails and updating records to enhance officer efficiency, the Government is simultaneously investing in skills development. This initiative aims to help officers collaborate effectively with AI and focus on higher-order responsibilities, such as analysing complex issues and designing solutions for citizens' needs. Specific HR policies for assisting officers made redundant by agentic AI adoption were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Detecting and Preventing Misuse of SkillsFuture Credits
results.summary: SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) enforces strict marketing guidelines prohibiting exaggerated claims, monetary incentives, and using credit expiry timelines to hard-sell courses. From 1 December 2025, registered training providers will be barred from engaging third parties to market courses directly to learners, addressing concerns over misrepresentation and data privacy. SSG supplements monitoring with public feedback and whistle-blowing, enforcing actions like registration suspension or termination for non-compliance, while publishing lists of penalized providers and course ratings on the MySkillsFuture Portal. Multiple reporting channels exist for complaints, though refunds are not guaranteed; SSG conducts regular audits to verify course quality and delivery.

results.title: Effectiveness of Nutri-grade Labelling System on Lowered Consumption of High-sugar and Saturated Fats Pre-packaged Foods
results.summary: Nutri-Grade measures, initially applied to beverages, have reduced median sugar levels from 7.1% in 2017 to 4.6% in 2023 and lowered average daily sugar intake from 60g in 2018 to 56g in 2022. From mid-2027, the system will extend to retail products high in sodium and saturated fat, including prepacked salt, sauces, seasonings, instant noodles, and cooking oil. Advertisements for these "D"-graded items will be prohibited across all media platforms. The government will continue monitoring effectiveness and regional developments before considering further actions.

results.title: Effectiveness of Penalties under Animals and Birds Act 1965 to Deter Animal Abuse
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the effectiveness of penalties under the Animals and Birds Act 1965 in deterring animal abuse was previously addressed in written replies to questions submitted by Mr Christopher de Souza, Mr David Hoe, Dr Charlene Chen, Mr Alex Yeo, and Mr Gabriel Lam on 22 September 2025 and 15 October 2025. The transcript does not contain the specific details of those replies or the Ministry's current methodology for incorporating research on links between animal abuse and psychological disorders into its determination process.

results.title: Employer's Awareness on Tripartite Standard on Unpaid Leave for Unexpected Care Needs
results.summary: From January 2023 to October 2025, 880 new employers adopted the Tripartite Standard on Unpaid Leave for Unexpected Care Needs, bringing total adopters to over 4,500. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and tripartite partners promote this through digital channels, SNEF workshops, and the biennial Tripartite Alliance Awards. While MOM has not yet published a public registry of adopters or reviewed the standard's sufficiency for pre-term infants, eligible parents can access up to 30 weeks of parental leave under recent enhancements. Additionally, the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests implemented in December 2024 provide a formal process for employees to request flexible work, allowing parents to balance caregiving with career aspirations. MOM will continuously monitor effectiveness and make adjustments as needed.

results.title: Employing AI Technologies to Review CCTV Recordings to Uncover Wrongdoing in Preschools
results.summary: The Minister stated that while the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) is developing AI solutions to detect safety hazards and misconduct for use in investigations, it does not mandate regular CCTV reviews by operators to avoid increasing administrative burdens. ECDA is collaborating with video analytics providers to create these AI tools, which may become pre-approved digital solutions under the Early Childhood Industry Digital Plan. Regarding tamper-proof systems, ECDA does not prescribe specific camera types but enforces strict compliance with footage submission requirements; failure to provide footage or tampering with recordings constitutes an offence under the Early Childhood Development Centres Act, punishable by fines up to $5,000 or imprisonment for up to six months. The agency emphasizes that CCTV is part of a broader safety suite including standard operating procedures and regular supervisory walkabouts.

results.title: Enforcing Penalties and Interventions against Repeated High-rise Littering Offences
results.summary: From 1 July 2023, the statutory presumption provision shifts the onus to flat owners and tenants to prevent high-rise littering, ensuring investigations proceed regardless of suspect cooperation. The National Environment Agency (NEA) issues advisories via Town Councils initially, then deploys surveillance cameras if littering persists. Convicted individuals face fines up to $2,000 for a first offence, $4,000 for a second, and $10,000 for third or subsequent convictions. Courts may also impose Corrective Work Orders requiring up to 12 hours of cleaning. For repeat offenders where mental health conditions contribute, NEA collaborates with grassroots leaders and families to provide support and implement preventive measures like installing wire mesh on window grilles.

results.title: Enhanced SGX-Listed Entities Disclosure Requirements for Foreign Regulatory Investigations and Settlements
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance stated that the question regarding additional disclosure requirements for SGX-listed entities involved in foreign regulatory investigations, settlements, or convictions was addressed in a reply at the Parliament Sitting on 5 November 2025. No new measures, timelines, or specific eligibility rules were announced in this session. The Government has not indicated any immediate changes to current disclosure frameworks for retail investors concerning overseas legal proceedings.

results.title: Ensuring Network Quality Maintenance with Greater Infrastructure Investment after Simba-M1 Consolidation
results.summary: The Ministry for Digital Development and Information states that the proposed Simba-M1 consolidation is being assessed by the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) under the Telecom and Media Competition Code. IMDA will review industry feedback, public input, and the entities' commitments to ensure service quality is maintained and competition is not significantly lessened. The Minister did not specify plans for mandatory infrastructure investment or unbundling requirements at this stage, noting that these aspects are part of the ongoing evaluation process.

results.title: Environmental and Biodiversity Studies of Forest along Sungei Serangoon in Light of Construction Works
results.summary: The Minister for National Development clarified that the proposed bus depot at Sungei Serangoon is located on a brownfield site zoned for industrial use since 1998, not near sensitive nature areas, rendering a full Environmental Impact Assessment unnecessary. While the Land Transport Authority will implement earth control and wildlife management measures to mitigate localized impacts, the site remains in a forested area that residents argue supports migratory bird connectivity. The Minister noted the site is not adjacent to residential zones, though MP Dennis Tan Lip Fong highlighted concerns regarding noise and dust affecting nearby condominiums like Kingsford Waterbay. Discussions are ongoing to address these disamenities, and the development aims to create a new job node for residents in Hougang, Sengkang, and future towns.

results.title: Evaluation on Vape Detection Sensors Usage in Schools and Institutes of Higher Learning
results.summary: The Ministry has not issued procurement guidelines or privacy safeguards for vape detection sensors. While some schools and Institutes of Higher Learning trialled these systems, deployment was not scaled up due to false positive issues. The Ministry will continue to assess promising technologies for effectiveness before considering wider deployment. Coordination with the Health Sciences Authority remains active for testing vapes suspected of containing etomidate.

results.title: Expanding Dental Services Offered At Polyclinics
results.summary: Polyclinic dental services already cover denture fabrication, repair, and fitting, with complex cases referred to tertiary institutions. The Ministry of Health recently increased subsidies for root canal treatments and permanent crowns. Effective 2026, MediSave claims for these specific treatments will be available to patients aged 60 and above once IT systems are configured. The Ministry will continue reviewing MediSave coverage to balance affordability with ensuring sufficient savings for future major acute healthcare needs in old age.

results.title: Expected Cost for Upcoming Free Off-peak Rides Pilot
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the expected cost and ridership impact projections for the upcoming free off-peak MRT and LRT pilot in northeast Singapore were already addressed during the 4 November 2025 Parliament Sitting. No new figures or timelines were provided in this session. The Minister directed the Member to refer to the previous response for details on the pilot's financial estimates and anticipated changes to commuter travel patterns. Further updates on the pilot's operational constraints or eligibility rules were not mentioned in this transcript.

results.title: Expected Layoffs in Petrochemical Industries following Retrenchment by US Energy Company of 500 Staff in Singapore
results.summary: The Minister clarified that ExxonMobil's restructuring involves retrenching up to 500 workers in Singapore by end-2027, noting that industry churn is expected during global transitions. While acknowledging potential layoffs, the Government highlighted over 30 new specialty chemical projects since 2021 expected to create more than 1,000 jobs in growth areas like bio-chemicals. For affected workers, the Taskforce for Responsible Retrenchment offers career matching, referrals to conversion programmes, and the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme providing up to $6,000 over six months. The Government will continue collaborating with companies to facilitate the transition to a low-carbon future, though no specific projection of further industry-wide layoffs was made.

results.title: Extending Clean and Green Singapore Day Events to More Residents
results.summary: NEA's Clean and Green Singapore (CGS) 2025 drive featured over 1,200 island-wide events with more than 400,000 participants, marking an increase from 400 events in 2023. The agency has no plans to set specific numerical targets for these campaigns, as the primary objective is to shape public attitudes toward sustainability rather than track absolute participation numbers for every event. While participation rates are tracked, NEA does not aim for a fixed target per event due to varying community needs. The agency is studying the feasibility of tracking carbon and energy savings at events, noting that developing such measurement capabilities is a growing skill needed across organizations to improve overall sustainability efforts.

results.title: Factors Leading to Higher Number of Babies in 2024 and Singapore's Long-term Parameter for Infrastructure Planning with Population Crossing Six Million-mark
results.summary: Citizen births reached 29,237 in 2024, a 1.2% rise driven largely by the dragon year effect among Chinese births, though this cultural influence is diminishing. Singapore's total population stood at 6.11 million in June 2025. Despite crossing the six million mark, the long-term planning parameter for infrastructure remains set at 6.9 million. Based on current trends, the population is projected to stay significantly below this 6.9 million threshold by 2030, confirming its continued relevance for planning throughout the 2030s.

results.title: First Responder Agency for Enforcing Late Night Noise Violations
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the specific first-response agency for enforcing late-night noise violations after 10.30 pm was not addressed in the current session. This query was previously answered in the Ministry of National Development's reply to Mr Dennis Tan Lip Fong on 25 September 2025. No new operational details, timelines, or agency designations were provided during this debate.

results.title: Framework and Duration for Vape Addicts' Treatment and Rehabilitation
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the National Addictions Management Service does not provide a specific framework or guaranteed duration for vape addiction treatment, as it is not a medical service. Appointments are scheduled strictly based on the availability of professionals rather than individual convenience. While the government enforces severe penalties for etomidate abuse, it offers a chance for young users to seek help voluntarily without enforcement action. Consequently, there is no scheduling flexibility to ensure continuity, and support depends entirely on the commitment of the individual and the availability of service providers.

results.title: Frequency of Maintenance of Nature Trails and Typical Downtime
results.summary: NParks inspects nature trails at least weekly, with additional checks following heavy rainfall or reported issues. Trails are temporarily closed solely for public safety or to facilitate maintenance, with closure duration varying based on the works' complexity. The Minister confirmed that trail closures are necessary measures and did not report specific delays in maintenance projects over the past five years.

results.title: Implementing Requirements for Dual Gender Supervision for Preschool Staff Interactions
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the ECDC Act and Regulations do not mandate the presence of both male and female staff during all preschool interactions. Instead, preschools must establish protocols ensuring activities are conducted by qualified, agency-approved staff who implement appropriate child management and safety practices. All staff, regardless of sex, are required to abide by these stipulated protocols when interacting with or caring for children. Consequently, the Ministry will not implement new protocols requiring dual-gender supervision as the current legal framework focuses on staff qualifications and safety measures rather than gender composition.

results.title: Institutionalising CCA Twinning to Enable Mixing of Students from Different Academic and Socio-economic Profiles
results.summary: Between 2019 and 2024, 18 co-curricular activity (CCA) partnerships were initiated by schools, all of which remain ongoing. While these multi-school activities incur higher costs and involve increased travel time, participating students reported positive experiences, developed specific skills, and forged cross-school friendships. The Ministry of Education supports schools keen on offering such activities and promoting social mixing, noting that sustainability depends on identifying suitable partners and overcoming coordination challenges. The Minister did not confirm plans to institutionalize these efforts beyond ad-hoc initiatives.

results.title: Introducing Clear Disclosure Requirements or Labelling Standards to Identify AI-generated Images on Products or Services
results.summary: The Ministry has no plans to introduce specific disclosure requirements or labelling standards for AI-generated content in products or services, as the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act already protects consumers against misleading claims regardless of AI usage. Instead, the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) is enhancing industry standards through updates to Technical Reference 76 and the release of an AI Markets Toolkit to help businesses self-assess fair trading risks. Consumer education remains a priority, with the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) conducting workshops for seniors and providing resources via the Digital for Life Portal to help the public identify misinformation and deepfakes. Existing frameworks like the Singapore Code of Advertising Practice continue to mandate that all advertisements remain decent, honest, and truthful.

results.title: Lapses Identified in Review Panel's Report on Fatal Child Abuse Incident
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the lapses in the 2020 Megan Khung case were isolated failures by specific officers rather than systemic issues, noting that the Investigation Officer resigned after internal discipline. While the Police do not track case closure durations due to varying complexities, they have implemented several enhancements since 2022, including the Family Violence Training Package and the establishment of Family Violence Teams at all Land Divisions. In 2023, the Domestic Violence Emergency Response Team (DVERT) was launched to provide round-the-clock on-site assessments and issue Emergency Orders under the Women's Charter for high-risk cases. Additionally, technological solutions now trigger automatic notifications to ensure time-sensitive tasks are completed promptly, while priority is accorded to missing vulnerable persons regardless of duration.

results.title: Managing National Food Stockpile while Being Mindful of Wastage, Nutritional Value and Food Prices
results.summary: The Government maintains national food stockpiles of rice, frozen protein, and canned vegetables as a resilience buffer, explicitly stating these are not used to influence market prices. The Singapore Food Agency conducts regular inspections and churning operations to ensure safety and minimise wastage, with operating costs borne by the Government. Alternative proteins are not currently stockpiled, and specific storage locations remain undisclosed due to national security concerns. While the Ministry works towards zero waste through commercial off-take and occasional large-scale humanitarian donations, it does not disclose detailed disposal strategies or audit frequencies for power supply measures.

results.title: Measurable Targets for Enhanced Comprehensive Strategic Partnership with Australia
results.summary: The Singapore-Australia Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (CSP) launched in 2015 expanded to CSP 2.0 last month, featuring 80 new initiatives across five cooperation pillars. Between 2015 and 2023, trade in goods rose 30% to $30 billion, services increased 170% to $50 billion, and Australian Foreign Direct Investment grew over 50% to $25 billion. The upgraded partnership includes an enhanced Defence Cooperation MOU granting reciprocal facility access and strengthening defence science, technology, logistics, and supply chain cooperation. Additionally, an MOU on Economic Resilience initiates negotiations for an Arrangement on Trade and Essential Supplies, with the Ministry exploring options for a future legally-binding commitment regarding essential supply chains.

results.title: Measures to Manage Price Volatility of Liquefied Natural Gas
results.summary: Singapore GasCo, established in April to centralise power sector gas procurement, will construct a diversified portfolio to mitigate price volatility and ensure no reliance on sanctioned entities. The entity will not disclose specific procurement details due to commercial sensitivities. A standby LNG facility, approved in November 2021 and operational by January 2022, successfully buffered against supply disruptions following the Ukraine conflict. Construction of a second LNG terminal commenced recently, with completion targeted between 2028 and 2030, which will increase storage and throughput capacity by approximately 50%. GasCo's Board oversees hedging strategies through a risk management committee, and diversified pricing is expected to stabilise costs from 2026 onwards.

results.title: Monitoring of Home-based Businesses to Ensure Fair Operations
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development (MND) clarified that Home-Based Business scheme regulations are proportionate to activity scale and externalities, differing from brick-and-mortar retailers. Unlike traditional retailers subject to stricter licensing, home-based businesses must maintain small-scale operations, are prohibited from displaying signages, and cannot hire personnel outside the household. These operational constraints ensure fair competition without granting unfair advantages. Members are directed to the MND's written reply dated 24 September 2025 for full regulatory details.

results.title: More Support for Workplace Leaders and Managers to Develop Competencies in Workplace Mental Well-being
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and the Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Council are encouraging employers to adopt initiatives that equip workplace leaders with competencies in self-care, stress resilience, and mental health awareness. Key measures include the recently launched *Handbook on Supporting Employees' Mental Health*, which guides senior management and supervisors in training to strengthen these specific competencies. Additionally, the Well-Being Champions Network has expanded from 54 founding member organisations in 2023 to 800 today, serving as a community of practice for leaders to share ideas on mental health management. MOM and the WSH Council will continue to facilitate employer participation in these relevant programmes to support a healthier workplace culture.

results.title: Name Correction of Berlayer Creek Mangrove Trail at Labrador Nature Reserve to "Berlayar" Creek
results.summary: The National Parks Board concluded its review in June and is progressively updating the name of the Berlayer Creek boardwalk at Labrador Nature Reserve to "Berlayar" Creek. This change aligns with the new Berlayar estate and historical place names in the area. The update includes modifications to official maps and signage. The Minister confirmed these actions are currently underway to reflect the corrected nomenclature.

results.title: Non-Stigmatising Terminology for Children Born out of Wedlock and Inheritance Parity Solutions
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development stated there are no plans to replace the legal term "illegitimate" with non-stigmatising alternatives like "non-marital child," as the concept remains established across statutes to reflect social norms promoting marriage. Under the Women's Charter, parents are legally obliged to maintain their children regardless of legitimacy status. Regarding inheritance, an illegitimate child is entitled to inherit from a mother who dies without surviving legitimate issue, while a mother can inherit from her illegitimate child as if the child were legitimate and she the sole surviving parent. Parents may also provide for such children through a will. The Ministry is not currently studying alternatives to adoption or marriage to secure parental responsibility or inheritance parity for children of single unwed mothers.

results.title: Opening Tengah Link to General Traffic during Off-peak Hours to Improve Connectivity
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that Tengah Link will remain a dedicated bus-only road and cannot be opened to general traffic, even during off-peak hours or as a pilot scheme. This decision is based on traffic modelling showing that allowing cars would lengthen bus travel times and create queues, as the single-lane road is not designed for mixed traffic. Furthermore, widening the road is not feasible due to existing infrastructure and utilities. While the Land Transport Authority has improved road markings and signages to guide motorists, the PIE interchange with Tengah Boulevard remains the primary alternative for vehicle access. The PIE interchange is scheduled for upgrades by 2028 to provide additional options as more bus services are introduced to the town.

results.title: Finance (Income Taxes) Bill
results.summary: The Government proceeds with implementing Pillar Two top-up taxes under the Finance (Income Taxes) Bill 2025 to avoid ceding revenue, despite the US withdrawal from BEPS 2.0, while monitoring global developments for potential recalibration. Rebates for eligible taxpayers are granted automatically, whereas retrospective exemptions require a formal application rather than an automatic refund mechanism. CPF contribution conditions apply solely to the cash grant component, excluding corporate income tax rebates, and securitisation entities face no liability for MNE unpaid taxes unless they are the sole Singapore constituent. The Bill serves as a procedural vehicle for Budget 2025 measures while refining the tax system for redistributive efficiency, though recurring one-off rebates are preferred over structural adjustments like indexing brackets. Concerns regarding R&D Incentive Credits being used as loopholes are addressed by requiring arm's length pricing for related party expenditures, and family office incentives remain subject to existing transparency rules.

results.title: Parties Seeking Endorsement under Healthier Food Donation Recommendation for Food Donation Packs
results.summary: There is no mandatory requirement for food donation packs to obtain Health Promotion Board (HPB) endorsement under the Healthier Food Donation Recommendation (HFDR). While HPB receives enquiries regarding guideline compliance, it does not certify parties or track endorsement counts. The Board encourages donors to include items from all main food groups, ensuring sufficient protein and fibre while limiting high-sodium, high-saturated-fat, and high-sugar discretionary foods like beverages, snacks, and sauces.

results.title: Peak Capacity of ActiveSG Gyms and Considerations for Building New Ones
results.summary: ActiveSG gym peak capacities are determined to maximise access while ensuring user space and fire safety compliance, with reviews conducted periodically. Larger gyms, such as Senja-Cashew and Clementi, experience higher visitorship relative to their size. The government plans at least one gym per residential town within ActiveSG Sport Centres, with existing facilities potentially expanding during renovations. For new constructions, the primary planning parameter requires a town sports centre with a gym in urban redevelopment authority areas with populations exceeding 130,000. While the government will continue reviewing different formats under the Sports Facilities Master Plan, establishing gyms in locations like shopping malls or community centres is not currently part of the planning parameters.

results.title: Plans for Additional Silver Zones
results.summary: There are no plans to designate additional Silver Zones beyond the current 50; instead, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has launched the Friendly Streets initiative to enhance safety for all residents. Introduced in 2023, this programme aims to expand to all towns by 2030, targeting high-pedestrian flow areas near amenities like MRT stations and hawker centres. Early pilots in five towns achieved a 20% drop in average vehicular speeds. Selection criteria focus on pedestrian activity levels, with specific measures such as signalised crossings and reduced speed limits implemented in areas like Punggol. Feedback from residents, including suggestions for Nee Soon South, will be considered as the LTA continues to identify suitable locations for implementation within each town.

results.title: Plans to Revise Minimum Annual Leave Entitlement in Section 88A of Employment Act
results.summary: The Minister confirmed no immediate plans to revise Section 88A of the Employment Act, which mandates a minimum of seven days' annual leave in the first year, increasing by one day per year of service up to 14 days. In 2024, over 90% of full-time resident employees aged 25 to 64 exceeded the statutory minimum, with 68.2% enjoying 15 days or more. While employers may offer additional leave, any future revisions will be part of a holistic tripartite review balancing worker protections against business costs. The current framework also considers other entitlements like paid public holidays and sick leave.

results.title: Prevention, Mitigation and Measures on Serious Child Abuse Cases
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development addressed queries regarding child abuse protocols, noting that questions on risk considerations for removing children, educator authority for medical assessments, and inter-agency search procedures for uncontactable caregivers were previously answered during the sitting on 5 November 2025. Additionally, the Minister referenced the Report on the Review Panel's Findings and Recommendations to Strengthen the Child Protection Ecosystem published on 23 October 2025. Specific details on investigative action differences between police reports dated 17 January 2020 and 20 July 2020 were not elaborated in this transcript. No new operational constraints, timelines, or eligibility rules were explicitly stated beyond these references.

results.title: Price Monitoring of EV Public Charging Services and Regulating Transparent and Affordable Pricing
results.summary: Charging prices for electric vehicles are commercial decisions varying by operator, location, and charger power rating. The Land Transport Authority monitors public charging prices and evaluated HDB carpark tenders based on competitive pricing among other factors. The Authority will introduce measures to foster competition if necessary to ensure fair and affordable pricing. No specific regulatory measures or price caps were announced in this session.

results.title: Proactive Measures of Primary Care Providers to Expand Vaccination and Antiviral Capacity
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) collaborates with general practitioners and polyclinics to integrate influenza vaccination into routine care, offering subsidies for eligible individuals including children, seniors, and those with medical conditions. Nursing homes are also engaged to vaccinate residents. Public hospitals manage daily high patient volumes and utilize alternative care models like Mobile Inpatient Care at Home (MIC@Home) for suitable patients and Transitional Care Facilities (TCFs) for medically stable patients to handle potential surges. These measures aim to expand vaccination and antiviral capacity while protecting vulnerable groups and managing hospital strain during the extended influenza season starting August 2025.

results.title: Processing Times of Traffic Police Summons and Preventing Repeat Offences and Licence Suspension
results.summary: The Traffic Police typically issues a Notice to Furnish Driver's Particulars within two weeks after verifying a speeding offence, though current processing times have extended to approximately one month due to a backlog from increased violations and a vendor ransomware attack. Owners must provide driver details within 14 days of the notice before a Notice of Traffic Offence is issued to the driver. The Ministry acknowledges these delays and is actively addressing the operational constraints to restore timely summons issuance and deter repeat offences.

results.title: Progress of Adopting Gen AI Tools within Public Service
results.summary: The Government has deployed AI tools in a secure environment, with approximately 80% of the 150,000 public officers utilizing Pair Chat and over 20,000 custom AIBots for specific tasks like HR queries and budget guidance. To support adoption, the Civil Service College and partners offer learning programmes covering prompt engineering and machine learning. Additionally, a mandatory AI literacy course was launched in October this year for all officers to understand AI's potential, limitations, and responsible usage.

results.title: Proportion of SkillsFuture Credits Courses Taken with Direct Relevance to Individuals' Ongoing or Future Employment
results.summary: The Ministry of Education clarified that SkillsFuture Credit usage is governed by specific conditions: the $4,000 Mid-Career top-up targets individuals aged 40 and above for curated courses with strong employment objectives, whereas the $500 opening credit aims to encourage lifelong learning across all life stages without requiring immediate employment relevance. Data indicates that eight in 10 learners find SSG-supported training content relevant to their work. The Government measures the culture of lifelong learning through course numbers, training provider participation, and demand metrics, while also analyzing job posting data and skills gaps alongside sector agencies to ensure training programmes address industry needs and facilitate career transitions.

results.title: Proposal for Permanent Baby Gift to Support New Parents
results.summary: The Ministry has no plans to permanently introduce a baby gift, as the SG60 and SG50 initiatives are commemorative milestones rather than ongoing policy measures. Consequently, the Government will not explore pilot programmes for specific regions, noting that Singapore's small size would make regional differentiation impractical and that cash grants like the Baby Bonus and First Step Grant better address parental needs. While the Minister stated she is not aware of any formal surveys on the impact of previous gifts, she acknowledged that parents often prefer cash to purchase specific essentials. The Government remains committed to reviewing existing financial support packages to ensure they remain relevant for supporting families.

results.title: Public Education Efforts on Nuclear Energy for Singapore
results.summary: The Government will conduct public education on nuclear energy benefits and risks, focusing on advanced technologies like small modular reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV reactors that offer enhanced safety and reduced accident impacts. While Singapore has ruled out conventional nuclear energy due to site constraints, it is assessing advanced options to diversify its energy mix and enhance security. Two teams—the Energy Market Authority's Nuclear Energy Office and the National Environment Agency's Nuclear Safety Division—are evaluating feasibility and safety, respectively, with the Singapore Nuclear Research and Safety Institute at NUS supporting simulations and public outreach. Regional neighbours plan nuclear deployment within 10 to 20 years, though specific timelines and technology types remain unconfirmed. The Government is training local talent and engaging with international partners, including the US, France, and the UK, to build capacity for potential future deployment.

results.title: Public Officers Registered as Well-Being@Gov Programme Users
results.summary: The Well-being@Gov programme, launched in March 2024, offers public officers on-demand access to well-being screening tools, self-care resources, and personalised coaching or counselling via a dedicated app. To date, over 100 agencies have onboarded officers, with approximately 10% actively using the app monthly; active users consume an average of 2.5 hours of coaching or five hours of counselling credits. The Public Service Division tracks efficacy through user satisfaction surveys, usage analytics, and agency feedback. Beyond the app, the Public Service maintains a network of roughly 1,800 Well-being Ambassadors for peer support and provides mental health first aid training for managers to foster workplace well-being.

results.title: Publication of Provisional Guidelines for Adequate Support Towards Workers in Pending Cases and/or Claims
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower confirms that the Employment of Foreign Manpower (Work Passes) Regulations require employers to provide acceptable accommodation and adequate food to Work Permit holders with pending claims. The Ministry proactively verifies these conditions throughout the claims process. Employers failing to comply may face financial penalties of up to $10,000. Concrete guidelines on these obligations are currently under consideration for publication.

results.title: Publishing Detailed Findings of Nuclear Technology Study
results.summary: The Government has not decided on deploying nuclear energy in Singapore, making discussions on specific sites, timelines, or reactor types premature. Dedicated nuclear teams at the Energy Market Authority and National Environment Agency are studying advanced technologies' safety and viability, excluding conventional large reactors deemed unsuitable. A background paper was published on 27 October 2025, and the Singapore Nuclear Research and Safety Institute conducts public dialogues to build understanding. Future plans depend on study results regarding commercial feasibility and supply chains, with potential updates to Parliament once findings are available.

results.title: Ratio of Teaching to Administrative Staff in Schools across Education Levels in Past Five Years
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding the ratio of teaching to administrative staff in primary, secondary, and post-secondary schools for the past five years was addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions Nos 2 to 5 on 4 November 2025. No specific numerical ratios or historical data were provided in this transcript. The response directs readers to the official parliamentary record for the detailed statistics.

results.title: Reducing Road Traffic Accidents and Fatalities Involving Motorcyclists and Pillion Riders
results.summary: Motorcyclists and pillion riders accounted for 54.7% of road accidents in the first half of 2025, with incident numbers rising 9.5% year-on-year. The Ministry of Home Affairs and Traffic Police maintain a zero-tolerance stance, aiming to prevent all fatalities through a combination of legislative penalties, public education, and enforcement. Recent initiatives include stricter helmet standards and the mandatory Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) for new motorcycles from 2027. While speeding-related accidents have declined, the government continues to review new technologies and upstream measures to address the disproportionate risk faced by motorcyclists.

results.title: Residential Noise Complaint Trends for Past Three Years and Comparison of WHO Standards with Singapore's Outdoor Noise Exposure Limits
results.summary: Residential construction noise complaints to the National Environment Agency (NEA) declined from an average of 1,040 per month in 2022 to 930 in 2024, while factory noise feedback remained stable at approximately 30 per month. NEA sets noise limits under the Environmental Protection and Management Act, referencing World Health Organization guidelines while accounting for local context, with strict enforcement against limit exceedances. The agency continues collaborating with stakeholders to review noise management approaches and reduce impacts from construction sites and factory premises. Specific WHO limit comparisons were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Review of Childcare Leave Entitlement Based on Number of Children under Seven Years Old
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated the Government will consider reviewing childcare leave provisions to calibrate entitlement based on the number of children under seven, rather than a flat rate. This suggestion will be taken into account during the next review of current leave provisions. Members are referred to the written reply to Parliamentary Question No 2 for the Sitting on 14 October 2025 for further details. The Government remains committed to supporting working parents in caring for their children.

results.title: Review on Employment Passes' Qualifying Salary Thresholds in Light of CPF Ordinary Wage Ceiling Increase in 2026
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) confirmed that Employment Pass qualifying salaries are benchmarked to the top one-third of resident wages, inclusive of CPF contributions, rather than specific wage components. Although the CPF Ordinary Wage ceiling increases on 1 January 2026, MOM reviews EP thresholds based on total resident wage trends and recently raised the salary in January 2025. The ministry will continue monitoring local wage trends to maintain a level playing field for locals and ensure sufficient adjustment time for employers during any future revisions.

results.title: Reviewing Nutritional Standards of Budget Meal Options
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment clarified that her Ministry does not oversee health and nutrition policies nor intends to regulate the nutritional value of hawker food. Instead, the National Environment Agency will collaborate with the Health Promotion Board to encourage healthier food options and choices at hawker centres. The Minister did not confirm plans to review nutritional standards for budget meal options or publish regular data on scheme accessibility, prices, composition, and participation rates as requested.

results.title: Reviewing Space Utilisation on New Buses to Accommodate More Assistive Devices
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority states that adding a third bay for wheelchairs, strollers, or personal mobility aids on new buses would require removing at least four seats per vehicle. This trade-off could reduce seating availability for non-mobility device users, including seniors and young children. Consequently, the Authority will study travel patterns and demand to determine if increasing dedicated space is the right solution or if suitable alternatives exist. No immediate change to current bus designs has been confirmed.

results.title: Roads with Three or More Lanes in Each Direction That Do Not Have Bus Lanes
results.summary: Approximately 80% of Singapore's roads with three or more lanes in each direction currently lack bus lanes. The Acting Minister for Transport stated the Government plans to reduce this proportion to prioritise public transport and reduce journey times for commuters. No specific timeline or detailed implementation strategy was provided in the response.

results.title: Safeguarding Singapore's Reputation as Clean International Financial Centre with Good Regulatory Standards
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated that the question regarding steps to safeguard Singapore's reputation as a clean international financial centre was previously addressed in a parliamentary reply on 5 November 2025. No new measures, timelines, or specific actions were announced during this sitting. The response indicates that the matter has already been handled in the prior session, and no further details were provided in this transcript.

results.title: Safeguards and Support for Frontline Professionals to Promote "Safe Reporting Culture" to Protect Children
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development noted that safeguards for frontline professionals regarding liability and legal challenges were addressed in an oral reply delivered on 5 November 2025. The response covered legal and institutional protections for teachers, social workers, and police officers making high-stakes child safety decisions, as well as steps to review past cases following the February 2020 incident. Measures to foster a "safe reporting culture" for social service professionals were also included in this consolidated reply. Specific details on the institutional protections and proactive review steps were not elaborated in the provided transcript excerpt.

results.title: Separate Sub-categories for Waste and Cleaning and F&B within Services Sector
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not currently establish distinct sub-categories for waste, cleaning, and food and beverage within the Services sector but regularly reviews Dependency Ratio Ceilings (DRCs) to align with economic needs and local workforce outcomes. While MOM acknowledges differing manpower requirements across sub-sectors, it provides targeted flexibilities for specific roles like landscaping and conservancy rather than creating separate quotas. The Ministry continues to review the Work Permit framework to drive productivity growth while calibrating it to support business needs without explicitly differentiating dependency ratios for the requested sub-categories.

results.title: Regulation of Imports and Exports (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Government supports the Regulation of Imports and Exports (Amendment) Bill to modernise enforcement, strengthen Trade Information Certificate (TIC) integrity, and expand Singapore Customs' investigative powers for a digital trading age. While addressing concerns about disproportionate burdens on SMEs and privacy, the Bill introduces a tiered enforcement framework where honest mistakes receive advisory notices rather than fines, and only "fit and proper" applicants are authorised to issue TICs. The legislation reinforces existing record-keeping requirements without adding new ones, ensuring proper Rules of Origin enforcement while safeguarding commercially-sensitive information. Singapore Customs will consider practical feedback from businesses and trade associations to mitigate adverse effects on trade competitiveness and prevent illicit transshipment activities.

results.title: SingLit Books Available for Purchase with SG Culture Pass Credits
results.summary: The Government is rolling out a scheme to allow SingLit books to be purchased with SG Culture Pass credits, introduced on 1 September 2025. Eligibility focuses on works written by locals rather than author nationality or publication location, though specific criteria are not yet finalized. The Ministry is collaborating with stakeholders to release detailed eligibility guidelines soon. The programme aims to enhance access to local arts and heritage for Singaporeans.

results.title: Statistics on Peer Support Student Leaders Identified and Trained in Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that schools train approximately 5% of their students as Peer Support Leaders (PSLs) using Ministry of Education resources. These leaders receive instruction in active listening, empathy, positive coping, and encouraging help-seeking to provide basic mental health support. While school staff regularly check in with PSLs to share good practices, training content may vary across schools depending on specific student profiles and needs. The transcript does not specify a standardisation mechanism to ensure consistency in training delivery.

results.title: Structured Review of International Educational Survey Findings and Implementation of Policy Recommendations
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that the Ministry of Education reviews findings from international surveys, including the OECD's Teaching and Learning International Survey (TALIS), alongside other data sources to inform policymaking and programme development. This structured process ensures insights from global benchmarks are integrated into local education strategies. The transcript does not specify a formal timeline for implementation or detailed eligibility criteria for these policy adjustments.

results.title: Successful Employment Rates of IMH's Job Club and Employment Outcomes
results.summary: Between 2012 and 2024, the Institute of Mental Health's Job Club facilitated 1,722 job placements for patients, averaging 132 annually. Of these placements, 46 individuals maintained employment in the same role for at least three years. The Minister noted that other patients sustained employment across various employers during the period. Specific percentages for those who cleared probation or remained employed after three years were not explicitly calculated or stated in the response.

results.title: Support Measures for Unsupervised Students during Home-based Learning Days
results.summary: Regular Home-Based Learning (HBL) Days apply only to secondary and pre-university students, who are deemed independent enough to learn without adult supervision. Primary schools conduct HBL selectively, prioritizing the need for adult supervision for younger students. For students lacking suitable home conditions or unavailable adult supervision, schools remain open to allow supervised HBL with teacher support. Parents receive advance notice of HBL dates to facilitate alternative care arrangements. The Ministry does not currently provide additional on-site options beyond these existing provisions for families unable to supervise younger children at home.

results.title: Targets and Timelines to Reduce Workload, Work Hours and Stress of Teachers
results.summary: The Minister for Education referred inquiries regarding quantitative targets and timelines to reduce teachers' administrative workload and stress to a previous response delivered on 4 November 2025. That prior response, which addressed OECD TALIS 2024 findings showing unchanged average work hours of 53 hours and 27% of teachers reporting high stress, will be available on the Ministry of Education's website. No new specific measures or revised timelines were announced in this current exchange.

results.title: Tracking Commuters Unable to Board First and Second Trains During Peak Hours at North-East Line
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority tracks passengers unable to board the first or second trains during peak hours on the North-East Line via regular surveys. Data indicates that less than 2% of commuters face boarding delays on the busiest sections, resulting in under four minutes of additional waiting time. The Ministry has not confirmed plans to release this specific dataset publicly.

results.title: Tracking Student Attendance Patterns and Identifying Causes of Absenteeism across Age Groups and Education Levels
results.summary: Under the Early Childhood Development Centres Act, all preschools must maintain daily attendance records, while the Ministry of Education tracks patterns across all schools. The Minister stated that most student absences are attributed to medical reasons. Specific data on absences by age group, including childcare centres, and detailed causes within each level were not provided in the response.

results.title: Tracking and Issuing Guidelines on Listed Prices for Subsidised Procedures at Public Hospitals
results.summary: The Ministry of Health provides broad guidance to Public Healthcare Institutions to set fees that recover operating costs after Government subsidies while maintaining care quality. Hospital bill information for surgical procedures and inpatient medical conditions is published on MOH's website. MOH clarified that Integrated Shield Plans and riders are primarily designed for private healthcare and have limited impact within the public healthcare sector. This position was confirmed in a written reply to Parliamentary Question 21 for the Sitting on 14 October 2025.

results.title: Unpaid Medical Bills Incurred by Work Permit and S Pass Holders in Public Healthcare Institutions
results.summary: Between 2022 and 2024, the Ministry of Manpower handled an average of 19 substantiated cases of unpaid medical bills by Work Permit and S Pass holders annually, with an average unpaid amount of $12,000. Employers are reminded to bear full treatment costs, with enforcement actions taken against non-compliant parties. Since 1 July 2023, the mandatory medical insurance minimum coverage increased from $15,000 to $60,000 per worker, covering approximately 99% of inpatient and day surgery bills. Starting 1 July 2025, insurers must make direct payments to hospitals upon claim admissibility, eliminating the need for employers to make upfront payments and seek reimbursement. Employers are encouraged to purchase additional coverage beyond the minimum to safeguard against higher medical expenses.

results.title: Update on NCSS' Job Fit Assessment and Referral Framework
results.summary: The National Council of Social Service (NCSS) will roll out a referral framework in June 2026 to provide customised employment support for jobseekers with mental health conditions through partnerships with Workforce Singapore and the Employment and Employability Institute. Concurrently, NCSS is standardising vocational assessment tools to evaluate work readiness and determine necessary support across agencies. The framework addresses a wide spectrum of conditions with individualised support durations based on professional assessments, aiming to equip individuals with skills for meaningful employment. Support measures may vary, as some individuals with unsuitable conditions may require alternative assistance rather than employment deployment. Specific eligibility criteria regarding job availability and exact support durations remain to be defined by the developing professional framework.

results.title: Update on Projects Involving Regional Importers Given Conditional Approvals or Licenses by EMA
results.summary: Since early 2023, the Energy Market Authority has issued Conditional Approvals to eleven regional import projects totaling eight gigawatts of low-carbon electricity from Australia, Cambodia, Indonesia, Malaysia, and Vietnam. Six Indonesian projects with three gigawatts capacity received Conditional Licences due to good progress. The Minister confirmed all companies remain interested in project completion. The Government will collaborate with source country governments and developers to conduct deeper viability assessments and support the most viable projects. No projects have been deemed unviable post-issuance.

results.title: Update on Renovation Works for Hougang Sports Centre and Possible Usage by Members of Public for Pickleball
results.summary: The Hougang Sports Centre is targeted for reopening by June 2026 following renovations that will expand facilities including a gym, fitness studios, and a sheltered teaching pool. The updated indoor sports hall will support activities such as basketball, badminton, and pickleball, with the latter explicitly available for public use. The centre will feature barrier-free access, sheltered drop-offs, and covered linkways to serve the broader community.

results.title: Waiting Time for Children and Youths Seeking Mental Health Support
results.summary: The median waiting time for mental health interventions via Youth Integrated Teams and CREST-Youth is approximately one week, with services currently limited to youths aged 12 to 25 years. Primary school children requiring addiction support are instead referred to REACH teams or community providers like TOUCH and WE CARE. The Ministry emphasizes upstream prevention through healthy lifestyle habits and cyber wellness lessons within the Character and Citizenship Education curriculum to address digital exposure. The Minister did not propose lowering the age limit for existing youth teams, noting that specialized referrals and school-based education remain the primary strategies for younger children facing digital challenges.

results.title: Weekday Commute Times Based on LTA's Latest Household Travel Survey Separated by Household Types and Income
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority's 2024 Household Travel Survey reports mean weekday door-to-door commute times to school between 30 and 35 minutes, and to work between 40 and 45 minutes. Median times are 25 to 30 minutes for school and 35 to 45 minutes for work. These figures apply across most housing types and income groups, with landed house residents showing slightly longer school commute times and the highest income band reporting slightly shorter work commute times. No significant trends were observed by housing or income categories otherwise.

results.title: Youth Unemployment Rates from 2000 to 2024 for Groups Aged 15 to 24 and 25 to 35
results.summary: From 2000 to 2024, youth unemployment rates for residents aged 15 to 24 and 25 to 34 have fluctuated with economic cycles but maintained a consistent relationship. In 2024, the rate stood at 6.8% for the younger group and 3.3% for the older group, levels comparable to 2000. The higher rate among new entrants reflects greater job search activity, yet Singapore's figures remain among the lowest internationally, ranking sixth lowest among OECD countries for youths and third lowest for those aged 25 to 34.

results.title: The Government’s Intention to Preserve the Site of 38 Oxley Road
results.summary: The Government intends to preserve the site of 38 Oxley Road as a National Monument under the Preservation of Monuments Act 2009 and acquire it for public use, aligning with recommendations from the National Heritage Board and Preservation of Sites and Monuments Advisory Board. While the site will not be redeveloped for private uses, no final decision has been made regarding a Preservation Order, and the Government has not yet issued one. The plan involves removing all traces of private living spaces to respect the wishes of Mr Lee and Mdm Kwa, without displaying or remodelling the interior. The Government aims to balance national significance with private preferences, ensuring sensitive handling of Mr Lee Kuan Yew's stance against personal monuments while avoiding misconceptions about memorialising individuals. Acquisition valuation transparency and consultation with residents remain pending as the Government evaluates future configurations, including potential conversion to a heritage park or integration with the Founders' Memorial.
